summary: Tested Minimal Outbox Lifecycle Fix feature. Backend: 9/9 pytest tests passed. Outbox worker successfully drains semantic events from pending to processed within ~2 seconds. Health score is GREEN with pending=0, processed=122. API returns lifecycle counts (pending/processing/processed/failed/parked) and ages. Frontend displays all lifecycle controls in Outbox tab with data-testid selectors.
features_tested: Temporary startup outbox worker drains semantic outbox events from pending to processed ✅
Outbox lifecycle state machine supports pending, processing, processed, failed, parked ✅
retry_count / processed_at / last_error / parked_at fields written correctly for failure paths ✅
GET /api/reports/migration-observability returns lifecycle counts and health score ✅
Frontend /app/migration-observability shows lifecycle controls panel in Outbox tab ✅
Creating a reservation via /api/pms/bookings creates outbox event that gets processed ✅
Health score is GREEN when pending=0, failed=0, stale_pending=0 ✅

issues: Console warnings about chart width/height being 0 or -1 during initial render and tab switches - non-blocking, charts display correctly after animations settle

critical_code_review_comments: OutboxLifecycleWorker implements atomic pending->processing claim via find_one_and_update with status filter
Worker uses MongoDB-based lock with expiry for single-runner assumption
Controlled drain with configurable poll_interval (2s), batch_size (10), drain_pause (0.1s)
Retry max 3 then parked: retry_count incremented on each failure, parked_at/parked_reason set on limit
Startup/shutdown hooks in server.py correctly start/stop the worker via @app.on_event decorators
Frontend Outbox tab shows all lifecycle states with proper data-testid attributes for testing

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all lifecycle UI elements render correctly, refresh stable)

seed_data_creation: Created test reservation via /api/pms/bookings to verify outbox event processing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Minimal Outbox Lifecycle Fix fully tested. Worker is operational and draining events from pending to processed. Health score shows GREEN. Processed count increased from 121 to 122 after creating a new reservation, confirming end-to-end flow works. Event breakdown table has all lifecycle columns. ModifyReservation is explicitly paused per CTO direction - only generic lifecycle is implemented, no domain-specific handlers yet.

e2e_flow_verified.result: Outbox event created with status=pending, then processed by worker within 4 seconds
